context:
<Profile>### IDENTITY
- **Name:** Mia Thompson  
- **Age:** 30  
- **Gender:** Female  
- **Experience level:** **Intermediate** — 2 years consistent/serious strength training; trains year-round for alpine ski racing (off-season build, in-season maintain).

---

### GOALS
- **Primary goal(s):** **Performance (alpine ski racing, masters/amateur)** — off-season emphasis (next ~8 months) on:
  - Leg power & explosive strength (jumping/plyometrics, Olympic-derivative work if used)
  - Single-leg strength (heavy unilateral patterns)
  - Knee/hip stability and injury prevention (especially right knee)
  - Core strength (anti-rotation/anti-extension; trunk stiffness under load)
- **Secondary goals:**
  - Maintain training consistency during off-season build
  - Improve movement quality and resilience for in-season demands (high eccentric loads, edge control)
- **Target events/deadlines:** Ski season begins in **~8 months (as of 2026-02-27)**

---

## TRAINING CONTEXT

### Schedule & Availability
- **Available training days:** **Mon / Tue / Thu / Sat**
- **Desired training frequency:** **4 sessions/week** (matches available days)
- **Session duration:** **60–75 minutes**
- **Time windows:** **Mornings** (exact time not specified)
- **Blocked days:** Wed/Fri/Sun (not specified why)

### Equipment & Environment
- **Training location:** **Hybrid** (commercial gym + home for quick sessions)
- **Commercial gym access:** Barbells, dumbbells, machines, plyo boxes (full gym setup implied)
- **Home equipment:** Some equipment available (not yet specified: exact items/loads)

### Constraints
- **[ACTIVE]** Previous right knee **MCL sprain** — currently managed with prehab/stability work; monitor valgus collapse/tibial rotation tolerance and symptom response, especially with cutting/jumping and deep knee flexion under load (**since 2026-02-27**, history; not marked resolved because ongoing management).

### Preferences
- **Likes / tends to prioritize:** Explosive movements, plyometrics, heavy single-leg work
- **Training style:** Off-season strength focus; in-season maintenance approach
- **Focus areas:** Single-leg strength, stability, injury prevention, core strength, explosive power

---

## METRICS
*(No objective performance metrics provided yet — add when available. Priority set to ski-performance-relevant strength/power first.)*

### Strength (as of 2026-02-27)
- **Not provided yet:** Key lifts/unilateral benchmarks (e.g., front squat/back squat, trap bar deadlift, split squat/Bulgarian split squat, RDL, step-up), and any rep/weight data.

### Endurance (as of 2026-02-27)
- Not provided / not prioritized (no current conditioning metrics supplied).

### Movement Quality (as of 2026-02-27)
- **Knee status:** History of right MCL sprain; currently able to train with prehab/stability (pain/instability levels not specified).
- **Not provided yet:** Single-leg squat quality, hop/landing mechanics, ankle dorsiflexion, hip IR/ER, trunk control tests.

### Body Composition (as of 2026-02-27)
- Not provided (weight, BF%, or trend not supplied).

---

## LOG
## 2026-02-27 — Profile created/updated (ski performance off-season focus)
- Set primary goal to **alpine ski racing performance** with **8-month off-season build** emphasis on leg power, stability, injury prevention, core strength, explosive power, and heavy single-leg strength.
- Confirmed training availability: **4x/week (Mon/Tue/Thu/Sat mornings), 60–75 min**.
- Equipment context added: **hybrid** training with **full commercial gym access** + unspecified home equipment.
- Constraint added: **[ACTIVE] prior right MCL sprain**, managed with prehab/stability work.

---  

If you share any current benchmarks (e.g., split squat 5RM per leg, trap bar deadlift, jump height/broad jump, or recent in-season notes), I’ll populate METRICS with dated entries for tracking.</Profile>

<Plan>## Header
- **Program name:** Alpine Ski Off‑Season Performance Build (Power + Single‑Leg Strength + Knee/Trunk Resilience)
- **Program Owner:** GymText
- **User:** Mia Thompson
- **Goal:** Alpine ski racing performance (off‑season build: explosive leg power, heavy single‑leg strength, knee/hip stability, trunk stiffness)
- **Duration:** **32 weeks (~8 months) to season start**, then transition to in‑season maintenance

---

## Program Philosophy
Mia is an intermediate lifter with a clear sport target (alpine ski racing) and a defined off‑season window, so this plan uses **block periodization** to build from movement capacity and tissue tolerance → maximal strength → power conversion → pre‑season robustness. The weekly structure matches her **4-day availability (Mon/Tue/Thu/Sat)** with two lower-body focused days (one power/strength, one unilateral/eccentric & stability) plus an upper/core day and a total-body power day to keep explosiveness and trunk stiffness progressing without excessive fatigue. Because of her **right MCL history**, the program prioritizes **landing mechanics, frontal-plane control, adductor/hamstring strength, and hip stability**, while managing exposure to deep knee flexion and high-rotation landings early on. Progression is **RPE-driven** (autoregulated) with planned deloads every 4th week; strength rises first, then power output becomes the primary KPI as season approaches.

---

# Phase 1 (Weeks 1–4): Foundation + Knee/Trunk Capacity (“Earn the Right to Go Heavy/Fast”)

### Weekly Pattern

**Mon — Lower Power Technique + Hinge Strength**
- **Focus:** Low-to-moderate intensity power exposures + posterior chain strength; landing mechanics
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets (plus short plyo/prep)

1) **Pogo jumps** (ankle stiffness): 3 × 20–30 sec @ smooth contacts  
2) **Snap-down to stick** (athletic landing): 3 × 3 @ controlled  
   *Builds braking/valgus control before higher-intensity plyos*  
3) **Hang power clean (from above knee) OR high pull**: 5 × 2–3 @ RPE 6–7  
   *Power patterning without grinding*  
4) **Trap bar deadlift** (or RDL if preferred): 4 × 5 @ RPE 7  
5) **Rear-foot elevated split squat (RFESS)**: 3 × 8/side @ RPE 7  
6) **Copenhagen side plank** (short lever to start): 2 × 15–25 sec/side  
   *Adductor capacity helps medial knee stability (MCL support)*

**Tue — Upper Strength + Trunk Anti-Rotation**
- **Focus:** Upper-body strength maintenance/gain + ski-relevant trunk stiffness
- **Volume:** ~16–20 working sets

1) **Bench press OR DB incline press**: 4 × 6 @ RPE 7  
2) **Weighted chin-up OR lat pulldown**: 4 × 6–8 @ RPE 7  
3) **1-arm cable row**: 3 × 10/side @ RPE 7  
4) **Half-kneeling landmine press**: 3 × 8/side @ RPE 7  
5) **Pallof press (tall kneeling)**: 3 × 10–12/side (2-sec hold each rep)  
6) **Dead bug (banded or weighted)**: 2 × 6–8/side (slow tempo)

**Thu — Unilateral Knee-Dominant + Eccentric Control**
- **Focus:** Single-leg strength, knee tracking, eccentric tolerance
- **Volume:** ~16–22 working sets

1) **Lateral bound to stick (small amplitude)**: 3 × 3/side  
   *Introduces lateral mechanics central to skiing while emphasizing control*  
2) **Front-foot elevated split squat** (controlled depth): 4 × 6/side @ RPE 7  
3) **Single-leg RDL**: 3 × 8/side @ RPE 7  
4) **Step-down (slow eccentric)**: 3 × 6/side @ RPE 6–7  
   *Targets knee control in a range you can own; regress height if valgus shows*  
5) **Hamstring curl (machine) OR slider leg curl**: 3 × 10 @ RPE 8  
6) **Tibialis raises + calf raises**: 2 × 12–20 each

**Sat — Total-Body Volume + Carry/Core**
- **Focus:** General strength base, core bracing under load, work capacity (not “conditioning burnout”)
- **Volume:** ~18–24 working sets

1) **Box squat to target depth** (or goblet squat if knee wants lighter): 4 × 6 @ RPE 7  
2) **DB bench OR push-ups weighted**: 3 × 10 @ RPE 7  
3) **Seated cable row**: 3 × 10 @ RPE 7  
4) **Hip thrust**: 3 × 8 @ RPE 7–8  
5) **Suitcase carry**: 4 × 20–30 m/side (heavy, crisp posture)  
   *Anti-lateral flexion strength for edge control/posture under perturbations*

### Progression Strategy (Phase 1)
- **Weeks 1–3:** Add **1 rep per set** or **2–5% load** week-to-week while keeping lifts mostly **RPE 6–7**.  
- **Plyos:** Progress amplitude slightly only if landings are quiet + knee tracks well; keep total contacts modest.  
- **Week 4 (Deload):** Reduce working sets by ~40–50%, keep load ~80–85% of week 3, keep plyos very light/technique-only.  
- **Transition criterion to Phase 2:** Consistent pain-free/controlled single-leg work, no next-day knee flare-ups, stable landings.

---

# Phase 2 (Weeks 5–12): Strength Base (Unilateral Emphasis) + Higher-Quality Power

### Weekly Pattern

**Mon — Lower Strength (Hinge) + Power**
- **Focus:** Strength foundation + low-fatigue power before heavy work
- **Volume:** ~16–20 working sets

1) **Countermovement jump (CMJ) OR trap bar jump (light)**: 4 × 2 @ RPE 6 (fast)  
2) **Trap bar deadlift**: 5 × 3–5 @ RPE 7–8  
3) **RFESS** (heavier): 4 × 6/side @ RPE 8  
4) **45° back extension OR RDL**: 3 × 8 @ RPE 7–8  
5) **Copenhagen plank** (progress lever): 2–3 × 20–30 sec/side

**Tue — Upper Strength + Trunk**
- **Focus:** Upper strength and scapular balance + anti-rotation/anti-extension
- **Volume:** ~16–22 working sets

1) **Bench press**: 5 × 4–6 @ RPE 7–8  
2) **Pull-ups/chin-ups (weighted if able)**: 5 × 4–6 @ RPE 7–8  
3) **DB shoulder press OR landmine press**: 3 × 8 @ RPE 7–8  
4) **Chest-supported row**: 3 × 8–10 @ RPE 8  
5) **Ab wheel rollout OR long-lever plank**: 3 × 6–10  
6) **Pallof press**: 2 × 10–12/side

**Thu — Lower Unilateral (Knee-Dominant) + Lateral Capacity**
- **Focus:** Heavy unilateral knee strength + controlled lateral work
- **Volume:** ~18–24 working sets

1) **Lateral bound to stick**: 4 × 3/side (increase distance gradually)  
2) **Front squat (if tolerated) OR safety bar squat OR hack squat**: 4 × 5 @ RPE 7–8  
   *Choose the most knee-friendly pattern; goal is strong legs without knee irritation*  
3) **Front-foot elevated split squat**: 4 × 5/side @ RPE 8  
4) **Step-up (knee above hip if tolerated)**: 3 × 6/side @ RPE 7–8  
5) **Hamstring curl**: 3 × 8–10 @ RPE 8  
6) **Hip airplane drill (bodyweight)**: 2 × 4–6/side  
   *Hip control to reduce knee collapse/tibial rotation*

**Sat — Power + Athletic Strength (Total Body)**
- **Focus:** Higher intent power + moderate strength; low grind
- **Volume:** ~16–22 working sets

1) **Hang power clean OR clean pull**: 6 × 2 @ RPE 7 (speed priority)  
2) **Box squat (speed focus)**: 6 × 2 @ ~60–70% (RPE 6–7, fast concentric)  
3) **Single-leg RDL**: 3 × 6–8/side @ RPE 7–8  
4) **Farmer carry**: 4 × 20–30 m (heavy)  
5) **Side plank row (cable/band)**: 2–3 × 8/side

### Progression Strategy (Phase 2)
- **Main lifts:** Use **double progression** within rep ranges (e.g., 5×3–5). When all sets hit top reps at target RPE, add **2–5%** load next week.  
- **Unilateral lifts:** Progress load first, then reps, while maintaining knee alignment (no valgus collapse).  
- **Deload:** **Week 8 and Week 12** — reduce sets ~40–50%, keep intensity moderate, keep power work technique-only.  
- **Transition criterion to Phase 3:** Strong, repeatable triples/fives at RPE 8 without form breakdown; plyos feel “snappy,” not knee-irritating.

---

# Phase 3 (Weeks 13–24): Max Strength + Power Conversion (Ski-Specific Force & Rate)

### Weekly Pattern

**Mon — Max Strength (Hinge) + Jumps**
- **Focus:** Heavy strength + minimal, high-quality jumps
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets

1) **Broad jump OR CMJ**: 5 × 1–2 (full rest)  
2) **Trap bar deadlift**: 6 × 2–3 @ RPE 8–9  
3) **RFESS**: 4 × 4–6/side @ RPE 8–9  
4) **Nordic hamstring (assisted if needed) OR ham curl**: 3 × 4–6 (or 3 × 8–10)  
5) **Copenhagen plank**: 2 × 20–40 sec/side

**Tue — Upper Strength + Bracing**
- **Focus:** Maintain upper strength; trunk stiffness under load
- **Volume:** ~14–20 working sets

1) **Bench press (or close-grip bench)**: 5 × 3–5 @ RPE 8  
2) **Weighted pull-up**: 5 × 3–5 @ RPE 8  
3) **1-arm DB row**: 3 × 6–8/side @ RPE 8  
4) **Overhead press (DB/barbell)**: 3 × 5–8 @ RPE 7–8  
5) **Front rack carry OR zercher carry**: 4 × 15–25 m  
   *Trunk stiffness and breathing/bracing under load (very ski-relevant)*

**Thu — Unilateral Strength + Lateral/Rotational Control**
- **Focus:** Heavy single-leg + lateral strength with strict mechanics
- **Volume:** ~16–22 working sets

1) **Lateral bound to stick OR skater hop to stick**: 5 × 2/side  
2) **Squat pattern (knee-friendly choice)**: 5 × 3–4 @ RPE 8–9  
3) **Front-foot elevated split squat**: 4 × 4–6/side @ RPE 8–9  
4) **Step-down** (add load if pristine): 3 × 5/side @ RPE 7–8  
5) **Pallof press (standing, step-out)**: 3 × 8/side (2-sec hold)

**Sat — Power Emphasis (Low Volume, High Intent)**
- **Focus:** Power output and speed-strength, minimal fatigue
- **Volume:** ~12–16 working sets

1) **Hang power clean**: 8 × 1–2 @ RPE 7–8 (fast)  
2) **Trap bar jump OR jump squat (light)**: 6 × 2 @ RPE 6  
3) **Med ball rotational scoop toss**: 4 × 3/side (full rest)  
   *Rotational power without knee-torquing pivots*  
4) **Suitcase carry**: 3 × 20–30 m/side

### Progression Strategy (Phase 3)
- **Strength lifts:** Gradually push intensity; aim to live mostly in **RPE 8–9** on heavy work while keeping reps low (2–4) and form strict.  
- **Power lifts/jumps:** Progress by **speed and crispness first**, load second. If bar speed slows or landings degrade, stop adding.  
- **Deload:** **Weeks 16, 20, 24** — reduce volume ~50%, keep a few heavy-ish singles/doubles at **RPE 7–8** to retain neural readiness.  
- **Transition criterion to Phase 4:** Strength is “there”; power sessions feel fast; joints feel good (especially right knee) with lateral hops.

---

# Phase 4 (Weeks 25–32): Pre‑Season Resilience + Power Maintenance (Durable Speed)

### Weekly Pattern

**Mon — Power + Strength (Minimal Effective Dose)**
- **Focus:** Keep strength and explosiveness without accumulating soreness
- **Volume:** ~12–16 working sets

1) **CMJ or broad jump**: 4 × 2  
2) **Trap bar deadlift**: 4 × 2 @ RPE 8  
3) **RFESS**: 3 × 5/side @ RPE 8  
4) **Copenhagen plank**: 2 × 20–30 sec/side  
5) **Calf/tib work**: 2 × 12–20

**Tue — Upper + Trunk**
- **Focus:** Maintain upper strength; trunk stiffness
- **Volume:** ~12–18 working sets

1) **Bench press**: 4 × 4 @ RPE 8  
2) **Pull-up**: 4 × 4–6 @ RPE 8  
3) **Row variation**: 3 × 8 @ RPE 7–8  
4) **Ab wheel / dead bug**: 3 sets

**Thu — Unilateral + Lateral (Quality > Quantity)**
- **Focus:** Ski-specific single-leg control and lateral force
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets

1) **Skater hop to stick**: 4 × 2/side  
2) **Squat pattern (knee-friendly)**: 3 × 3 @ RPE 8  
3) **Front-foot elevated split squat**: 3 × 5/side @ RPE 8  
4) **Step-down OR sled drags (backward)**: 3 × 6/side or 6–10 min  
   *Backward sled is knee-friendly quad work and builds tendon tolerance*

**Sat — Power “Primer” + Mobility/Recovery Bias**
- **Focus:** Stay snappy; leave the gym fresh
- **Volume:** ~10–14 working sets

1) **Hang power clean OR high pull**: 6 × 1–2 @ RPE 7  
2) **Trap bar jump (light)**: 5 × 2  
3) **Med ball throws (rotational + overhead)**: 6–10 total throws  
4) **Carry (optional)**: 2–3 short heavy trips

### Progression Strategy (Phase 4)
- Keep strength within **90–95% of Phase 3** while reducing volume.
- Power stays frequent but low-dose; stop sets when speed drops.
- **Week 32 taper into season:** reduce lower-body volume another ~30–40%, keep 1–2 fast power exposures.

---

## Phase Cycling
- **Order:** Phase 1 (Foundation) → Phase 2 (Strength Base) → Phase 3 (Max Strength + Power Conversion) → Phase 4 (Pre‑Season Maintenance/Taper)  
- **Deload rhythm:** Every 4th week on average (explicit deloads embedded per phase).  
- **After week 32 (in-season concept):** Shift to **2–3 days/week maintenance**, keeping:
  - 1 lower strength exposure (heavy but low volume)
  - 1 power/plyo primer (very low contacts)
  - 1 optional upper/core session as schedule allows  
- **Trigger to repeat/extend phases (future years):**
  - If knee tolerance is limiting → extend Phase 1–2 style work
  - If strength is limiting but joints are healthy → extend Phase 2–3
  - If strength is solid but explosiveness is flat → increase Phase 3–4 power emphasis

---

## Modification History
- **2026-02-27:** Initial program created based on Mia’s off-season ski performance goals, 4-day schedule (Mon/Tue/Thu/Sat), and right MCL management needs.

--- 

If you share (1) which lifts you prefer for squat pattern (front squat vs hack squat vs safety bar), (2) what you have available at home, and (3) any current split squat / trap bar numbers, I can tighten loading targets and choose the most knee-friendly squat/plyo progressions for you.</Plan>

<Week># Microcycle — Week of 2026-02-23

**Program:** Alpine Ski Off‑Season Performance Build (Power + Single‑Leg Strength + Knee/Trunk Resilience)  
**Phase:** Phase 1 — Foundation + Knee/Trunk Capacity (Week 1 of 4)  
**User:** Mia Thompson  

## Schedule
- **Mon (2/23):** Lower Power Technique + Hinge Strength (commercial gym, morning)
- **Tue (2/24):** Upper Strength + Trunk Anti‑Rotation (commercial gym, morning)
- **Wed:** Off
- **Thu (2/26):** Unilateral Knee‑Dominant + Eccentric Control (commercial gym, morning)
- **Fri:** Off
- **Sat (2/28):** Total‑Body Volume + Carry/Core (commercial gym, morning)
- **Sun:** Off

## Week Overview
Week 1 of Phase 1 is about **clean movement reps and knee/trunk ownership**, not maxing out. Keep the explosive work **snappy and low-fatigue**, and keep single-leg work **strictly aligned** (tripod foot, knee tracks over 2nd–3rd toe; no valgus collapse). Because you didn’t provide current numbers yet, loads below are **conservative “Week 1 starters”**—keep them if they land at the prescribed RPE; otherwise adjust **same day** to hit the target effort.

---

# Workout — Monday, February 23, 2026
**Focus:** Lower power technique + hinge strength; landing mechanics  
**Location:** Commercial gym  
**Duration:** ~70 minutes  

## Warm-Up (12 minutes)
1. **Bike (easy nasal breathing):** 4 minutes  
2. **Dynamic leg swings:** 10 forward/back + 10 lateral each leg  
3. **Glute bridge:** 2 × 10 (2-sec squeeze)  
4. **Mini-band lateral walk:** 2 × 10 steps each direction  
5. **Snap-down rehearsal (no jump):** 2 × 3 (pause 2 sec in athletic position)

## Main Workout

### 1. Pogo Jumps
**Target:** 3 × 20 sec @ smooth, quiet contacts  
- **Set 1:** 20 sec  
- **Set 2:** 20 sec  
- **Set 3:** 20 sec  
**Rest:** 45–60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Ankles stiff, knees “soft,” torso tall—stop if contacts get loud or sloppy.

### 2. Snap-Down to Stick
**Target:** 3 × 3 @ controlled (2-sec stick)  
- **Set 1:** 3 reps  
- **Set 2:** 3 reps  
- **Set 3:** 3 reps  
**Rest:** 60–90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Land with feet under hips, ribs down, knees track forward (no cave-in on the right).

### 3. Hang Power Clean (from above knee)
**Target:** 5 × 3 @ RPE 6–7 (speed priority)  
- **Set 1:** 45 lb × 3 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 55 lb × 3 (warm-up)  
- **Set 3:** 65 lb × 3 (RPE ~6)  
- **Set 4:** 65 lb × 3 (RPE ~6)  
- **Set 5:** 70 lb × 3 (RPE ~6.5)  
- **Set 6:** 70 lb × 3 (RPE ~6.5)  
- **Set 7:** 70 lb × 3 (RPE ~7)  
**Rest:** 2 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Think “jump + fast elbows.” If you’re catching with crashing wrists or slow turnover, reduce 5–10 lb.

### 4. Trap Bar Deadlift
**Target:** 4 × 5 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 95 lb × 5 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 135 lb × 5 (warm-up)  
- **Set 3:** 155 lb × 3 (warm-up)  
- **Set 4:** 175 lb × 5 (RPE ~6.5)  
- **Set 5:** 180 lb × 5 (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 6:** 180 lb × 5 (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 7:** 180 lb × 5 (RPE ~7–7.5)  
**Rest:** 3 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Wedge in (lats tight), push the floor away, finish tall without over-leaning back.

### 5. Rear-Foot Elevated Split Squat (DB)
**Target:** 3 × 8/side @ RPE 7  
- **Warm-up:** Bodyweight × 6/side  
- **Set 1:** 25 lb DBs (50 total) × 8/side (RPE ~6.5)  
- **Set 2:** 30 lb DBs (60 total) × 8/side (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 3:** 30 lb DBs (60 total) × 8/side (RPE ~7–7.5)  
**Rest:** 90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Slight forward torso, front foot tripod, knee tracks clean. Cut depth slightly if the right knee feels “pinchy” or unstable.

### 6. Copenhagen Side Plank (short lever)
**Target:** 2 × 20 sec/side  
- **Set 1:** 20 sec/side  
- **Set 2:** 20 sec/side  
**Rest:** 45–60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Hips stacked, top hip slightly forward; don’t rotate open.

## Cool Down (6 minutes)
1. **Hip flexor stretch:** 45 sec/side  
2. **Adductor rock-backs:** 8 reps/side (slow)  
3. **90/90 breathing on back:** 5 slow breaths  

## Notes
- Aim: leave feeling **springy**, not smoked.  
- Knee check: no medial knee discomfort during split squats; if present next time, we’ll reduce ROM and bias more hinge volume.

---

# Workout — Tuesday, February 24, 2026
**Focus:** Upper strength + trunk anti‑rotation/anti‑extension  
**Location:** Commercial gym  
**Duration:** ~65–70 minutes  

## Warm-Up (10 minutes)
1. **Row erg (easy):** 3 minutes  
2. **Band pull-aparts:** 2 × 12  
3. **Scap push-ups:** 2 × 8  
4. **Half-kneeling cable chop (light):** 1 × 8/side (prime trunk control)

## Main Workout

### 1. Barbell Bench Press
**Target:** 4 × 6 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 45 lb × 10 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 65 lb × 6 (warm-up)  
- **Set 3:** 80 lb × 6 (warm-up)  
- **Set 4:** 95 lb × 6 (RPE ~6.5)  
- **Set 5:** 100 lb × 6 (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 6:** 100 lb × 6 (RPE ~7–7.5)  
- **Set 7:** 100 lb × 6 (RPE ~7.5)  
**Rest:** 2–3 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Feet planted, slight arch, pause 1 sec on chest if shoulders feel cranky (keeps it honest and stable).

### 2. Lat Pulldown (neutral or supinated grip)
**Target:** 4 × 8 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 70 lb × 8 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 90 lb × 8 (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 3:** 90 lb × 8 (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 4:** 95 lb × 8 (RPE ~7–7.5)  
- **Set 5:** 95 lb × 8 (RPE ~7.5)  
**Rest:** 90 sec between working sets  
**Notes:** Drive elbows to ribs; don’t turn it into a low-back lean.

### 3. 1-Arm Cable Row
**Target:** 3 × 10/side @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 30 lb × 10/side (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 2:** 30 lb × 10/side (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 3:** 35 lb × 10/side (RPE ~7–7.5)  
**Rest:** 60–75 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Square hips/shoulders (no twisting)—this is sneaky anti-rotation work.

### 4. Half-Kneeling Landmine Press
**Target:** 3 × 8/side @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 35 lb (bar + load) × 8/side (RPE ~6.5)  
- **Set 2:** 40 lb × 8/side (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 3:** 40 lb × 8/side (RPE ~7–7.5)  
**Rest:** 75–90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Glute of down-knee squeezed; ribs down; press up-and-forward.

### 5. Pallof Press (tall kneeling, 2-sec holds)
**Target:** 3 × 10/side  
- **Set 1:** 20 lb × 10/side  
- **Set 2:** 20 lb × 10/side  
- **Set 3:** 25 lb × 10/side  
**Rest:** 45–60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Don’t let the cable pull you into rotation—stay stacked.

### 6. Dead Bug (weighted)
**Target:** 2 × 6/side (slow tempo)  
- **Set 1:** 10 lb DB held over chest × 6/side  
- **Set 2:** 10 lb DB held over chest × 6/side  
**Rest:** 45 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Low back stays gently glued to floor; exhale fully on each rep.

## Cool Down (5 minutes)
1. **Doorway pec stretch:** 30 sec/side  
2. **Lat stretch (hanging or bench stretch):** 30 sec/side  
3. **Thoracic rotations (open books):** 6/side  

## Notes
- You should finish feeling like you could do “a bit more”—that’s perfect for Week 1.

---

# Workout — Thursday, February 26, 2026
**Focus:** Unilateral knee-dominant + eccentric control; lateral mechanics  
**Location:** Commercial gym  
**Duration:** ~70–75 minutes  

## Warm-Up (12 minutes)
1. **Bike (easy):** 4 minutes  
2. **Ankle rocks (knee over toes):** 10/side  
3. **Bodyweight reverse lunge:** 1 × 6/side  
4. **Mini-band monster walk:** 2 × 8 steps forward/back  
5. **Step-down practice (low box):** 1 × 5/side (slow lower)

## Main Workout

### 1. Lateral Bound to Stick (small amplitude)
**Target:** 3 × 3/side @ controlled stick (2 sec)  
- **Set 1:** 3/side  
- **Set 2:** 3/side  
- **Set 3:** 3/side  
**Rest:** 60–90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Think “push sideways, land quiet.” Knee tracks; pelvis level.

### 2. Front-Foot Elevated Split Squat (DB, controlled depth)
**Target:** 4 × 6/side @ RPE 7  
- **Warm-up:** Bodyweight × 6/side  
- **Set 1:** 25 lb DBs (50 total) × 6/side (RPE ~6.5)  
- **Set 2:** 30 lb DBs (60 total) × 6/side (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 3:** 30 lb DBs (60 total) × 6/side (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 4:** 30 lb DBs (60 total) × 6/side (RPE ~7–7.5)  
**Rest:** 90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Use a 2–3" plate for elevation. 2-sec lower, brief pause at bottom if stable.

### 3. Single-Leg RDL (DB)
**Target:** 3 × 8/side @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 35 lb DB × 8/side (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 2:** 35 lb DB × 8/side (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 3:** 40 lb DB × 8/side (RPE ~7.5)  
**Rest:** 75–90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Hips stay square; reach back with the free heel; slight knee bend on the stance leg.

### 4. Step-Down (slow eccentric)
**Target:** 3 × 6/side @ RPE 6–7  
- **Setup:** 6" box/step  
- **Set 1:** Bodyweight × 6/side (RPE ~6)  
- **Set 2:** 10 lb DB (goblet) × 6/side (RPE ~6.5)  
- **Set 3:** 10 lb DB (goblet) × 6/side (RPE ~6.5–7)  
**Rest:** 60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** 3-sec lower, light toe tap, stand back up without bouncing. Regress height if the right knee drifts inward.

### 5. Seated or Lying Hamstring Curl (machine)
**Target:** 3 × 10 @ RPE 8  
- **Set 1:** 50 lb × 10 (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 2:** 55 lb × 10 (RPE ~8)  
- **Set 3:** 55 lb × 10 (RPE ~8)  
**Rest:** 60–75 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Full curl, controlled return (don’t let it slam).

### 6. Tibialis Raises + Standing Calf Raises
**Target:** 2 × 15–20 each  
- **Tibialis raise:** 2 × 15 (bodyweight, back against wall)  
- **Standing calf raise:** 2 × 15 @ 50 lb (machine or DBs)  
**Rest:** 45–60 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Tib work matters for skiing shin demands and knee tracking via ankle control.

## Cool Down (6 minutes)
1. **Adductor stretch (side lunge hold):** 30 sec/side  
2. **Quad stretch:** 30 sec/side  
3. **Calf stretch:** 30 sec/side  
4. **Easy walk:** 2 minutes

## Notes
- This is the “knee ownership” day. If the right knee feels even mildly unstable, prioritize **perfect reps** and reduce load 5–10 lb immediately.

---

# Workout — Saturday, February 28, 2026
**Focus:** Total-body volume + carry/core; general strength base  
**Location:** Commercial gym  
**Duration:** ~70 minutes  

## Warm-Up (10 minutes)
1. **Incline treadmill walk:** 4 minutes  
2. **Goblet squat (light):** 1 × 8 @ 20 lb  
3. **Hip hinge drill (dowel or empty hands):** 1 × 8  
4. **Plank:** 1 × 20 sec (brace practice)

## Main Workout

### 1. Box Squat to Target Depth (barbell)
**Target:** 4 × 6 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 45 lb × 8 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 65 lb × 6 (warm-up)  
- **Set 3:** 85 lb × 4 (warm-up)  
- **Set 4:** 105 lb × 6 (RPE ~6.5)  
- **Set 5:** 110 lb × 6 (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 6:** 110 lb × 6 (RPE ~7–7.5)  
- **Set 7:** 110 lb × 6 (RPE ~7.5)  
**Rest:** 2–3 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Box is a depth target, not a crash pad—sit back under control, stay braced, stand up fast.

### 2. Dumbbell Bench Press
**Target:** 3 × 10 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 35 lb DBs × 10 (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 2:** 35 lb DBs × 10 (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 3:** 40 lb DBs × 10 (RPE ~7.5)  
**Rest:** 90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Slight pause at bottom; shoulder blades stay pinned.

### 3. Seated Cable Row
**Target:** 3 × 10 @ RPE 7  
- **Set 1:** 70 lb × 10 (RPE ~6.5–7)  
- **Set 2:** 75 lb × 10 (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 3:** 75 lb × 10 (RPE ~7–7.5)  
**Rest:** 75–90 sec between sets  
**Notes:** Tall posture; finish with elbows back, not shoulders shrugged.

### 4. Barbell Hip Thrust
**Target:** 3 × 8 @ RPE 7–8  
- **Set 1:** 95 lb × 8 (warm-up)  
- **Set 2:** 115 lb × 8 (RPE ~7)  
- **Set 3:** 125 lb × 8 (RPE ~7.5)  
- **Set 4:** 125 lb × 8 (RPE ~7.5–8)  
**Rest:** 2 minutes between working sets  
**Notes:** Chin tucked, ribs down; full lockout with glutes, not low back.

### 5. Suitcase Carry
**Target:** 4 × 25 m/side (heavy, crisp posture)  
- **Set 1:** 55 lb DB × 25 m/side  
- **Set 2:** 55 lb DB × 25 m/side  
- **Set 3:** 60 lb DB × 25 m/side  
- **Set 4:** 60 lb DB × 25 m/side  
**Rest:** 45–60 sec between trips  
**Notes:** Don’t lean—zipper up (ribs stacked), slow controlled steps.

## Cool Down (6 minutes)
1. **Figure-4 glute stretch:** 30 sec/side  
2. **Hamstring stretch:** 30 sec/side  
3. **Child’s pose + side reach (lats):** 30 sec/side  
4. **Breathing (90/90):** 5 slow breaths  

## Notes
- This is the higher-volume day—still no grinding. If squats feel knee-irritating, shorten ROM slightly and keep RPE closer to 6.5–7.

---

## Weekly Summary
- **Mon (Lower power + hinge):** Introduced pogo/snap-down + hang power clean technique; hinge strength anchored by trap bar deadlift at **180×5** working sets (conservative Week 1 start).
- **Tue (Upper + trunk):** Bench volume at **100×6** and solid trunk control work (Pallof + dead bug) to build ski-relevant stiffness.
- **Thu (Unilateral + eccentric):** High-value knee control session—split squat + step-down eccentrics; keep right knee tracking pristine before increasing height/load.
- **Sat (Total-body + carries):** Box squat volume at **110×6** and heavy suitcase carries (up to **60 lb**) for anti-lateral flexion.
- **Constraint monitoring (right MCL history):** This week keeps plyo amplitude modest and emphasizes controlled sticks/eccentrics. Any next-day medial knee soreness >2/10 = reduce split squat depth and step-down height next week.
- **Next week (Phase 1, Week 2):** If RPEs were on target and knee feels good, add **+5 lb** to trap bar and box squat working sets, and **+2.5–5 lb per DB** on split squats (or add **1 rep/set** if load jumps feel too aggressive).

If you tell me (1) your current best guess for trap bar deadlift 5RM, (2) what DBs you used recently for RFESS, and (3) whether front squats usually bother your knee, I’ll tighten Week 2 loading so it’s dialed-in instead of conservative.</Week>
